briefdescription.para: Wait for future types and retrieve their values.

detaileddescription.para: This syntax is most useful for cases where we are immediately requesting the future result.
The function also makes the syntax optionally a little closer to languages such as javascript.

simplesect.para: This function only participates in overload resolution if all types are futures.
The result of the future object

briefdescription.para: Wait for future types and retrieve their values as a tuple.

simplesect.para: This function only participates in overload resolutions if all types are futures.
The result of the future object

briefdescription.para: Wait for a sequence of futures to be ready.

detaileddescription.para: This function waits for all futures in the range [first, last) to be ready. It simply waits iteratively for each of the futures to be ready.

simplesect.para: This function is adapted from boost::wait_for_all

parameterdescription.para: Iterator type in a range of futures
Iterator to the first element in the range
Iterator to one past the last element in the range

briefdescription.para: Wait for a sequence of futures to be ready.

detaileddescription.para: This function waits for all futures in the range r to be ready. It simply waits iteratively for each of the futures to be ready.

simplesect.para: This function is adapted from boost::wait_for_all

briefdescription.para: Wait for a sequence of futures to be ready.

detaileddescription.para: This function waits for all specified futures fs... to be ready.
It creates a compile-time fixed-size data structure to store references to all of the futures and then waits for each of the futures to be ready.

simplesect.para: This function is adapted from boost::wait_for_all

briefdescription.para: Wait for a sequence of futures to be ready.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::chrono::duration< Rep, Period > &timeout_duration, Iterator first, Iterator last)
memberdef.name: wait_for_all_for

briefdescription.para: Wait for a sequence of futures to be ready.

parameterdescription.para: Iterator type in a range of futures
Iterator to the first element in the range
Iterator to one past the last element in the range

simplesect.para: std::future_status::ready if all futures got ready. std::future_status::timeout otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Wait for a sequence of futures to be ready.

simplesect.para: std::future_status::ready if all futures got ready. std::future_status::timeout otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Wait for a sequence of futures to be ready.

simplesect.para: std::future_status::ready if all futures got ready. std::future_status::timeout otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Wait for a sequence of futures to be ready.

simplesect.para: std::future_status::ready if all futures got ready. std::future_status::timeout otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::chrono::time_point< Clock, Duration > &timeout_time, Iterator first, Iterator last)
memberdef.name: wait_for_all_until

briefdescription.para: Wait for a sequence of futures to be ready.

parameterdescription.para: Iterator type in a range of futures
Iterator to the first element in the range
Iterator to one past the last element in the range

simplesect.para: std::future_status::ready if all futures got ready. std::future_status::timeout otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Wait for a sequence of futures to be ready.

simplesect.para: std::future_status::ready if all futures got ready. std::future_status::timeout otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Wait for a sequence of futures to be ready.

simplesect.para: std::future_status::ready if all futures got ready. std::future_status::timeout otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Wait for a sequence of futures to be ready.

simplesect.para: std::future_status::ready if all futures got ready. std::future_status::timeout otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Wait for any future in a sequence to be ready.

detaileddescription.para: This function waits for any future in the range [first, last) to be ready.
Unlike wait_for_all, this function requires special data structures to allow that to happen without blocking.

For disjunctions, we have few options:
If the input futures support external notifiers:
Attach continuations to notify when a task is over
If the input futures do not have lazy continuations:
Polling in a busy loop until one of the futures is ready
Polling with exponential backoffs until one of the futures is ready
Launching n continuation tasks that set a promise when one of the futures is ready
Hybrids, usually polling for short tasks and launching threads for other tasks
If the input futures are mixed in regards to lazy continuations:
Mix the strategies above, depending on each input future
If the thresholds for these strategies are reasonable, this should be efficient for futures with or without lazy continuations.

simplesect.para: This function is adapted from boost::wait_for_any

parameterdescription.para: Iterator type in a range of futures
Iterator to the first element in the range
Iterator to one past the last element in the range

simplesect.para: Iterator to the first future that got ready

briefdescription.para: Wait for any future in a sequence to be ready.

detaileddescription.para: This function waits for any future in the range r to be ready. This function requires special data structures to allow that to happen without blocking.

simplesect.para: This function is adapted from boost::wait_for_any
Iterator to the first future that got ready

briefdescription.para: Wait for any future in a sequence to be ready.

detaileddescription.para: This function waits for all specified futures fs... to be ready.

briefdescription.para: Wait for any future in a tuple to be ready.

briefdescription.para: Basic function to wait for futures.

detaileddescription.para: This module defines a variety of auxiliary functions to wait for futures.
